plan.title: Gerlach Law — Site Status & Growth Plan
plan.subtitle: Live Site + 12-Month Growth Plan
plan.audience: For Angela E. Gerlach, Esq. · Fort Lauderdale, FL
plan.author: Evans Diaz · DCF
plan.pitchDateLabel: Live since Jul 13, 2026 · Growth plan locked Jul 12
plan.milestonesTitle: The Next 90 Days — Post-Launch Punch List
plan.contact: Angela E. Gerlach, Esq. · Angela@gerlachlegal.com · Yuly Fiallo (assistant) · (954) 233-0755
plan.heroHeadline: Gerlach Law is |live — the growth plan starts now.
plan.heroSub: gerlachlegal.com went live July 13 on a static passthrough build. The launch blockers are cleared; what's left is the go-live kit and the 12-month growth plan already locked.

auditFindings.label: gerlachlegal.com — post-launch status, 2026-07-17
auditFindings.intro: The site is genuinely live and verified working — the open items now are the go-live kit and a few client-side decisions, not a broken build.
auditFindings.sourceLine: Source: live re-check of gerlachlegal.com, 2026-07-17, and the 2026-07-12 engagement audit.

findings.title: Zero testimonials collected
findings.detail: The site has no client reviews or testimonials live anywhere, and none have been collected yet through a formal ask.
findings.fix: Stand up a review-request flow the same way it's built for Robert Davis — day-0 ask, day-3 follow-up.
findings.evidence: Zero testimonials collected as of the 2026-07-12 engagement audit — no request flow has been run yet.

findings.title: Brand font mismatch, print vs. web
findings.detail: Print materials use Cormorant; the live site uses Playfair. Same brand, two different typefaces depending on where you see it.
findings.fix: Standardize on one typeface across both, or confirm the print/web split is intentional.
findings.evidence: Print collateral uses Cormorant; gerlachlegal.com uses Playfair Display — flagged as unresolved in the 2026-07-12 audit.

findings.title: Site verified live and clean
findings.detail: gerlachlegal.com returns HTTP 200 with the correct Gerlach Law title, zero WordPress or Divi markers, and Cloudflare serving the static build correctly — re-checked 2026-07-17.

findings.title: GA4 setup has zero blockers on DCF's side
findings.detail: The GA4 property setup is entirely DCF-side work with nothing waiting on Angela — it's a matter of scheduling it, not chasing anyone.

phases.sub: Go-Live Kit — DCF Side, Zero Blockers
items: GA4 property ID swapped into the live site
Google Search Console + Google Business Profile fields confirmed
Client dashboard (built Jul 10) shared with Angela for the first time
Logo PNG/SVG from Yuly for the final brand pass
Cal.com hours confirmed (5pm→6pm) and paid-vs-free consult direction decided
Final balance and Phase 2 retainer pricing reconciled together
Review-request flow launched — zero testimonials today, target a real number within the first quarter
Brand font standardized across print and web

phaseProof: The launch blocker turned out to be a misdiagnosis, not a real wall — the DNS was never the issue, and the site has been live and verified since July 13. What's left is a short, named list: three things on DCF's side with zero blockers, three things waiting on a decision from Angela or Yuly. Nothing here is open-ended.
closingPoints: gerlachlegal.com is live, verified, and clean — re-checked July 17, HTTP 200, zero legacy WordPress markers.
The remaining punch list is short and named, not vague — the GA4 swap and the dashboard share are entirely on DCF's side with nothing blocking them.
Three items sit with Angela and Yuly: the logo files, the Cal.com hours decision, and reconciling the final balance with Phase 2 retainer pricing.
The 12-month growth plan is already locked from the July 12 engagement audit — it starts executing the moment the punch list above clears.
Robert Davis P.A. is running the same playbook one quarter ahead — what works there, like the review-request flow and the content cadence, transfers directly here.

openQuestions.q: Should the final balance and the Phase 2 retainer be priced and signed together in one conversation, or handled separately?
openQuestions.why: Both have been open since before launch — batching them into one ask, the same way it's working for Robert Davis, is faster than two separate follow-ups.

offerIntro: Not a new pitch — this is what's contracted, what's still open, and what Phase 2 would add once it's priced.

offerTiers.name: Phase 1 — Contracted
items: Static site live since July 13, verified clean
Balance and final pricing pending reconciliation with Angela

offerTiers.name: Phase 2 — Growth Plan
items: Review-request engine, same model built for Robert Davis
Brand consistency pass — one typeface across print and web

offerWhyNow: The site has been live for over a week with the growth plan already scoped and waiting — the only thing between here and Phase 2 starting is a short list of named decisions, not new work.

activityLog.label: Added to the Plan of Action Hub as a full plan
activityLog.detail: Gerlach Law upgraded from a roster link-out to a full in-hub plan — audit, milestones, offer, and dashboard — same system as every other plan in the hub.

activityLog.label: Infra health sweep — re-verified live
activityLog.detail: gerlachlegal.com confirmed HTTP 200, correct title, zero WordPress/Divi markers, 58 KB static homepage, Cloudflare serving the static build correctly.

activityLog.label: gerlachlegal.com goes live
activityLog.detail: 7-week DNS blocker resolved — misdiagnosed; no DNS change was needed. Passthrough theme solved the index.php/index.html conflict on GoDaddy Managed WordPress. Site live serving gerlach-v8.3 (commit b1c40e0).

activityLog.label: Full engagement audit + 12-month growth plan locked
activityLog.detail: Engagement at ~31% complete, one blocker (DNS) identified and later resolved. 12-month growth plan logged to the Notion Master Plan session log.
